author: itsdodobitch
message:
Speed up terminal color picker by caching derived values

TerminalAppearanceEditor's 200-swatch color matrix caused noticeable
scroll jank in Settings because swiftUIColor wrapped an NSColor on
every access (Color(nsColor:) is a dynamic, identity-keyed box), and
hexString was recomputed via String(format:) for every cell on every
body evaluation.

Switch swiftUIColor to the static Color(red:green:blue:) initializer
so SwiftUI can diff cells by value and skip unchanged swatches, and
compute hexString and hsb once at init time. Persisted themes keep
loading: Codable is restricted to red/green/blue via CodingKeys and
derived values are recomputed on decode.

author: itsdodobitch
message:
Speed up Kanban view by caching filter results

The Kanban view felt sluggish on initial load and especially when
toggling the workbench primary column. Root cause was a combination of
three patterns unique to Kanban among the workbench views:

1. The `filteredTasks` computed property ran the full board filter
   (including `matchesSearch`, which builds a 10+ string haystack per
   task and folds each one) nine times per body evaluation: once in
   `boardContent`, once per status in `kanbanGroupedList` (7x), and
   once in `panelTitle`. The body was re-evaluated multiple times
   during the implicit collapse animation in
   `HermesCollapsibleHSplitView`, multiplying the cost.

2. `kanbanGroupedList` wrapped its status groups in a regular VStack,
   forcing SwiftUI to build the view tree for every task card up front.
   The inner LazyVStack only helped with rendering, not construction.

3. `DateFormatters.relativeFormatter()` instantiated a new
   RelativeDateTimeFormatter on every call with no caching, and was
   invoked from inside `KanbanTaskCard.metadataStrip` — once per card
   per render.

Cache the four expensive computed properties
(`filteredTasks`, `displayStatuses`, `assigneeOptions`,
`tenantOptions`) in @State and recompute them via a single
`recomputeKanbanCaches()` triggered by `.onAppear` and `.onChange`
on the six inputs (`kanbanBoard`,
`includeArchivedKanbanTasks`, `statusFilter`, `assigneeFilter`,
`tenantFilter`, `searchText`). The existing onChange handlers for
include-archived and status-filter are preserved and combined with the
recompute to avoid duplicates.

Switch the outer container of `kanbanGroupedList` from VStack to
LazyVStack so off-screen status groups are not constructed.

Cache `DateFormatters.relativeFormatter()` in the per-thread
dictionary, matching the pattern already used by
`shortDateTimeFormatter()`.

No behavior change: same SSH-first flow, same UI, same data path. All
112 tests pass.

author: itsdodobitch
message:
Fix unreliable window title bar configurator

The previous HermesWindowTitleBarConfigurator scheduled its titleVisibility
update via DispatchQueue.main.async and relied on view.window being
non-nil at that point. In practice the NSView was still orphan when the
async block fired, so the guard returned early and the native
'Hermes Desktop' title from WindowGroup remained visible next to the
toolbar's collapse buttons.

Replace the async-dispatch pattern with an NSView subclass that applies
titleVisibility = .hidden in viewDidMoveToWindow, where AppKit
guarantees self.window is non-nil. The configurator now attaches
reliably on first appearance and on every subsequent re-attach
(state restoration, window recreation).

author: itsdodobitch
message:
Refresh site hero and harden release packaging

- Rewrite site/index.html hero with the new layout and content
- Reject placeholder bundle versions in package-github-release.sh
  so archives cannot ship with CFBundleShortVersionString == 0.0.0
- Ignore .deriveddata-* scratch directories in .gitignore

author: itsdodobitch
message:
Sync localizations after removing chat and overview

Drop strings that backed the deleted Hermes chat and overview tab
across the English, Russian, and Simplified Chinese tables. Keeps
LocalizationCoverageTests passing.

author: itsdodobitch
message:
Remove Hermes chat and overview surfaces from desktop

Drop the HermesGateway-backed chat plumbing and the overview tab that
fronted it from the macOS app.

- Delete HermesChatService, HermesGatewayChatService, and their tests
- Delete OverviewView and the now-unused HermesChatModels
- Trim AppState (-1069 lines) and SessionDetailView (-860) so the
  app no longer composes, persists, or renders chat sessions
- Update AppSection, RootView, HermesDesktopCommands, and
  ConnectionsView to drop the chat/overview navigation entries
- Adjust AppSectionTests, ConnectionProfileTests, and
  HermesChatInvocationTests for the removed APIs

author: edoardododorico
message:
Polish UI across views

Refresh shared layout primitives in HermesUI.swift and apply the
updated styling to cron jobs, files, Kanban, sessions, skills, usage,
and workflows for a more consistent surface.
